findings.message: The new `create_item` route bypasses the repository layer and talks to the persistence layer directly: it imports the `SessionLocal` session factory from `..db.engine`, opens its own session, and constructs/persists the `Item` ORM object inline. This is a layering violation — every other route here goes through the repository (`list_items`) and obtains its session via the `get_session` FastAPI dependency. A `create_item(session, name)` function already exists in `..db.repository` for exactly this purpose, so the route is duplicating data-access logic that belongs behind the repository boundary and coupling the HTTP layer directly to engine internals.
findings.suggestion: Inject the session via the existing `SessionDep`/`get_session` dependency and delegate to the repository: `def create_item(session: SessionDep, payload: ItemCreate) -> Item: return repository.create_item(session, payload.name)`. Drop the `SessionLocal` import from the route module so the routes layer no longer reaches into the engine's session factory. (Note: the repository's `create_item` also commits, which the inline version omits — another reason to route through it.)
